title: Unit 04 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Civil Liberties, Civil Rights, and Law.

questions.prompt: What is the Bill of Rights?

choices.text: The first ten amendments to the Constitution
choices.feedback: Correct. The Bill of Rights includes the first ten amendments.

choices.text: A list of laws created by Congress
choices.feedback: Incorrect. The Bill of Rights is part of the Constitution.
Incorrect. It is a set of constitutional amendments.
Incorrect. It applies to the national Constitution.

questions.prompt: Civil liberties protect individuals from government interference.

choices.feedback: Correct. Civil liberties protect freedom from government action.
Incorrect. That is the definition of civil liberties.
Incorrect. That is a civil liberty.
Correct. Civil rights protect against discrimination.
Incorrect. Civil rights protect individuals, not government power.
Incorrect. That is not related to civil rights.

questions.prompt: Due process requires the government to follow fair procedures.

choices.feedback: Correct. Due process ensures fairness in procedures.
Incorrect. That is the definition of due process.

choices.text: The government can treat people differently without reason
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Equal protection requires fairness.

choices.text: The law must be applied fairly to people
choices.feedback: Correct. Equal protection ensures fair treatment.
Incorrect. Equal protection applies broadly.
Incorrect. Courts interpret laws.

questions.prompt: Which situation is an example of a civil rights issue?

choices.feedback: Incorrect. That is a civil liberty.

choices.text: A person denied access because of race
choices.feedback: Correct. That is discrimination, a civil rights issue.
Incorrect. That is a civil liberty.

choices.text: A person voting in an election
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Voting is civic participation.

questions.prompt: Rights are always unlimited and cannot be restricted.

choices.feedback: Incorrect. Rights can be limited in certain situations.
Correct. Rights can be limited to protect safety and order.

questions.prompt: Why might the government limit a right?

choices.text: To protect public safety and order
choices.feedback: Correct. Limits are used to protect society.
Incorrect. Rights are protected, not removed.
Incorrect. Limits must follow the Constitution.

choices.text: To give one group all power
choices.feedback: Incorrect. Limits are not for unequal control.
